articles/getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | Resultify
articles/getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started This guide shows how to quickly install and use the Resultify NuGet package in your .NET project. 1. Install via NuGet You can install the package using the .NET CLI: dotnet add package Monadion.Resultify --version 0.1.0 Or via Visual Studio: Right-click on your project → Manage NuGet Packages… Search for Monadion.Resultify Click Install 2. Add a Using Statement In your C# code, add: using Monadion.Resultify; 3. Example Usage Here’s a simple example of how to use the package: using Monadion.Resultify; Result result = Result.Success(); if (result.IsSuccess) { Console.WriteLine("result success"); }

articles/introduction.html.title: Introduction | Resultify
articles/introduction.html.summary: Introduction Welcome to the Resultify documentation! Resultify is a lightweight, fluent, functional-style Result library for C#. It provides Result and Result<T> with Try, Map, Bind, and Match to build safe, exception-free, chainable pipelines. Features Fluent API: Try, Map, Bind, Match Result and Result<T> Exception-safe execution via Result.Try(...) Designed for clean, chainable functional pipelines Why Resultify? Safer error handling: Avoid exceptions in normal control flow. Readable code: Compose operations with Map/Bind instead of nested if checks. Consistent outcomes: Model success and failure explicitly with a single return type. Getting Started Start with Getting Started (installation + first usage), then explore: Examples for common patterns and pipelines API Reference for the full surface area

articles/samples.html.title: Samples | Resultify
articles/samples.html.summary: Samples All samples below assume: In your C# code, add: using Monadion.Resultify; Sample 1 — Success (non-generic) Result result = Result.Success(); if (result.IsSuccess) { Console.WriteLine("result success"); } Sample 2 — Failure (non-generic) Error error = new Error("Error.Code", " error description"); Result result = Result.Failure(error); if (result.IsFailure) { Console.WriteLine($"result failure | code: {result.Error.Code}, description: {result.Error.Description}"); } Sample 3 — Success (generic) Result<int> result = Result<int>.Success(1); if (result.IsSuccess) { Console.WriteLine($"result success | value:{result.Value}"); } Sample 4 — Failure (generic) Error error = new Error("Error.Code", "error description"); Result<int> result = Result<int>.Failure(error); if (result.IsFailure) { Console.WriteLine($"result failure | code: {result.Error.Code}, description: {result.Error.Description}"); } Sample 5 — Try + Match (exception-safe) Result<int> .Try(() => { throw new DivideByZeroException(); }) .Match( success => Console.WriteLine($"success: {success}"), failure => Console.WriteLine($"failure: {failure.Description}") );

index.html.title: Resultify | Resultify
index.html.summary: Resultify Resultify is a lightweight, fluent, functional-style Result library for C#. It provides Result and Result<T> with Try, Map, Bind, and Match to build safe, exception-free, chainable pipelines. Install dotnet add package Monadion.Resultify Quick Example var message = Result.Try(() => int.Parse(input)) .Map(x => x * 2) .Match( onSuccess: v => $"OK: {v}", onFailure: e => $"ERR: {e}" ); Features Fluent API with Try, Map, Bind, Match Non-generic and generic Result types Exception-safe operations with Result.Try Designed for chainable functional pipelines Next steps Getting Started: articles/getting-started.md Examples: articles/examples.md Changelog: ../CHANGELOG.md
